model.label: Qwen 3.5 Flash
model.description: Alibaba • Suitable for simple tasks, fast and low cost.

pick.rationale: Louisville holds a commanding advantage as a top-20 team ranked 16th in Torvik and 18th in KenPom compared to South Florida's 50th rankings in both systems. The Cardinals' elite offensive rating (124 adjO, top 25 nationally) combined with solid defensive efficiency (98-99 adjD, top 25) creates a clear mismatch against an American Athletic Conference team whose average metrics don't translate to tournament success. With nearly double the efficiency margin (+25.42 vs +15.77) and proven Power 5 conference pedigree, Louisville's superior talent and system depth make them the clear favorite to advance past the 11-seed.

reasoningStep.title: Louisville vs South Florida Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Advanced metrics show Louisville significantly outperforms South Florida across all categories despite USF's slightly better record.
reasoningStep.evidence: Louisville ranks 16th/18th in Torvik/KenPom with elite offense (124 adjO, 20th-23rd nationally) and solid defense (98-99 adjD, 23rd nationally). South Florida ranks 50th in both systems with average metrics (117-118 adjO, 58th-65th; 102-103 adjD, 60th). Louisville's efficiency margin of +25.42 exceeds USF's +15.77 by nearly 10 points per 100 possessions. Historical 6-seed vs 11-seed matchups favor the higher seed approximately 70% of the time.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

2. Search for any recent news about injuries or team status
3. Consider the matchup dynamics

This is a 6/11 seed matchup, where historically 6 seeds win about 70% of the time against 11 seeds.

Let me start by getting the ratings for both teams.

modelTrace.content:
The search tools are consistently failing with 403 errors. I have enough data from the CBB ratings to make a well-informed pick. Let me analyze what I have:

Louisville (6-seed):
- Torvik rank: 16th, KenPom rank: 18th
- Record: 23-10
- AdjO: ~124 (elite - top 25 nationally)
- AdjD: ~98-99 (solid - top 25 nationally)
- Efficiency margin: +25.42 (excellent)
- ACC conference (Power 5)

South Florida (11-seed):
- Torvik rank: 50th, KenPom rank: 50th
- Record: 24-8 (better record but weaker conference)
- AdjO: ~117-118 (decent - around 60th nationally)
- AdjD: ~102-103 (average - around 60th nationally)
- Efficiency margin: +15.77 (good but not elite)
- American Athletic Conference (mid-major)

Key factors:
1. Louisville is ranked nearly 35 spots higher in both rating systems
2. Louisville's efficiency margin is about 10 points better per 100 possessions
3. Louisville has elite offense and solid defense; USF has average offense and defense
4. Louisville plays in the stronger ACC conference
5. Historically, 6 seeds win about 70% of games against 11 seeds

USF does have a better record (24-8 vs 23-10), but that comes from the weaker American Athletic Conference. The advanced metrics clearly favor Louisville across the board.

I should pick Louisville with moderate-to-high confidence given the significant metric gap, though 11 seeds do occasionally pull off upsets.

Good, I've confirmed the game details. Now I have all the information I need to make a pick:

Louisville (6-seed) vs South Florida (11-seed):
- Louisville ranks 16th/18th in Torvik/KenPom vs USF's 50th/50th
- Louisville has elite offense (~124 adjO, top 25) and solid defense (~98-99 adjD, top 25)
- USF has average offense (~117-118 adjO, ~60th) and defense (~102-103 adjD, ~60th)
- Louisville's efficiency margin (+25.42) is about 10 points better than USF's (+15.77)
- Louisville plays in the stronger ACC conference
- Historically 6 seeds win ~70% of games against 11 seeds

The advanced metrics show a clear advantage for Louisville across every category. While USF has a slightly better record (24-8 vs 23-10), that comes from the weaker American Athletic Conference. The talent gap shown by the ratings suggests Louisville should handle this matchup comfortably.

I'll pick Louisville with moderate-high confidence (around 70-75%) given the significant metric disparity but acknowledging that 11 seeds do occasionally upset.
